description:
Create a new model in SketchUp Make 2017 to detail an exterior conduit run for a solar installation.

Instructions:
1. Draw a building volume: A rectangular box 20 feet wide × 20 feet deep × 10 feet high.
2. Create a 'Junction Box' on the roof: A small rectangle (approx 6"x6") located on the top face, 5 feet back from the front edge.
3. Create an 'Inverter' on the front wall: A box (approx 2'x3') located on the front vertical face, with its bottom 3 feet above the ground.
4. Model the Conduit Path: Draw a line/path representing the conduit run. It must start at the Junction Box on the roof, run along the roof surface to the front edge, go over the edge, run down the front wall, and end at the top of the Inverter.
5. Create 3D Conduit Geometry: Create a circular profile (approx 1-2 inches diameter) at the start of the path. Use the Follow Me tool (or manual extrusion) to extrude this circle along the entire path. The result should be a continuous solid tube connecting the roof box to the wall box.
6. Group the conduit geometry and save the model to 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\conduit_detail.skp'.
